milestone: feat/ocr-layout-complexity-v1 (Auto OCR routing: Layout Complexity v1)
phase: A (frozen candidate list, no bytes fetched yet)
purpose: The existing OCR Auto Policy v1 calibration corpus (PR #101) lacks representative figure-heavy scientific content. Its 21-doc set is heavy on scanned synthetics, single-page ParseBench excerpts, and one local GeoTopo sample; it does not span multi-column arxiv-style preprints with dense figure/table structure.

This lockfile pins a small, public, reproducibly-fetchable set of scientific PDFs that will be used in Commit 3 (calibration) to test whether the layout-complexity detector separates 'simple digital' from 'structurally complex' correctly on real scientific content.

No bytes are fetched at this phase. Every ``expected_sha256`` and ``expected_size_bytes`` is null; they will be populated by an authorised fetch step in Commit 3 (mirroring the ParseBench Phase B2 pattern).

redistribution_posture.reason: arXiv preprints are publicly fetchable at deterministic URLs but come with per-paper licences that vary (arxiv perpetual licence, CC-BY, CC-BY-NC-SA, etc.). Reference-fetch matches ParseBench's posture and avoids assuming a universal redistribution grant.

fetch_convention.url_verification: The arXiv PDF endpoint is deterministic for a pinned (arxiv_id, arxiv_version) tuple. Fetch step MUST verify that the fetched bytes have the sha256 recorded here once populated; a mismatch is an integrity failure and MUST NOT populate the calibration corpus.

assets.title: Attention Is All You Need
assets.why_included: Canonical two-column ML paper. Several small architecture diagrams and multi-cell tables; a control for 'multicolumn with moderate figure density' that should trigger the multi-column and figure-caption signals without producing extreme image-area-ratio numbers.

assets.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
assets.why_included: Two-column paper with many small figures and results tables. Higher figure density than the Attention paper; tests whether the detector separates 'moderate figures' from 'figure-heavy' when both are multi-column.

assets.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
assets.why_included: Two-column NLP paper dominated by result tables (dozens of tables comparing benchmark scores). Tests the table_count and rejected_table_candidate_count signals in a document where tables — not figures — should drive complexity.

assets.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
assets.why_included: Two-column paper with a high density of inline and display equations. Tests the math_bbox_count signal on a real doc where math regions dominate structural complexity, not figures or tables.

assets.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
assets.why_included: Long two-column paper with heavy mixed figure and table density plus appendices. Tests whether the detector remains stable on a substantially longer document than the others (48 pages vs 12-25), and whether appendix pages (often single-column or lighter layout) dilute the document-level signal.

not_included_yet.reason: Deferred to a later iteration. The corpus above is deliberately arxiv-preprint-focused because that is where 'figure-heavy scientific' concentrates; a single-column control is nice to have but not load-bearing for the initial detector calibration.

Scanned scientific PDFs would exercise the ocr_pixmap + figure-caption interaction more strongly than the arxiv preprints above, but no obvious public reproducible source exists that would clear rights review without individual per-document evaluation. Left out of v1.

future_authorised_steps: Commit 3 (calibration): fetch each asset via its deterministic pdf_url into a user cache root OUTSIDE the repo tree, sha256 the fetched bytes, populate expected_sha256 and expected_size_bytes in a follow-up lockfile update, and add each asset as a CorpusEntry with source='science' in the calibration harness.
Any subsequent addition to the corpus (new arxiv paper, or an authorised non-arxiv source) MUST be a separate lockfile edit reviewed by the same rights posture that governs ParseBench.
